title: Support simulation context for concurrent/asynchronous simulations
body:
First, thanks a lot for this great and very powerful Verilog/SV simulator!

We are currently integrating Verilator into DaCe as part of the RTL CodeGen backend (https://github.com/spcl/dace). For that, we would like to simulate multiple tasklets (Verilog/Verilator design) concurrently, in separate threads. This works well until we add support for tracing, see https://github.com/spcl/dace/pull/490. 

The main problem is, that Verilator expects a single global `double sc_time_stamp()` in https://github.com/verilator/verilator/blob/b684995292b8943c944850b7b9eef03ecbe18912/include/verilated.h#L862 function, which is not scalable to multiple concurrent simulations. 

Is there a way to overcome this limitation with the current implementation of Verilator?  

Otherwise, possible solutions would be:
- Add a parameter to the `double sc_time_stamp()` function indicating where the call comes from (i.e. in my case top0 or top1). 
- Make the function dependent on the design name i.e. `double sc_time_stamp_top0()`
- Add a function pointer parameter to the initialization routine, such that the user can write her/his own routine and passes it.

I attached a minimal working example that should work out-of-the box i.e `cmake . && make && ./simulation`

[multi_tasklet_issue_example.zip](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/files/5622887/multi_tasklet_issue_example.zip)
